role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified the absence of linguistic cues (no signs) and stayed within domain.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Analyzed terrain, soil, and climate cues consistent with its role.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Analyzed vegetation types (scrub, pines) consistent with its role.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed road markings and infrastructure consistent with its role.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized infrastructure and visual quality cues consistent with its role.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Descriptions of scrub, soil, and hills match the image perfectly.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: Descriptions of sclerophyllous scrub and pines match the image.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Descriptions of road markings and poles match the image.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Descriptions of infrastructure and camera quality match the image.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents provided high-quality, visually grounded assessments but suffered from a severe lack of geographic diversity, converging almost entirely on the Northern Hemisphere (Spain/Portugal/Greece). The judge failed to identify the critical missing evidence (driving side) and the Southern Hemisphere possibility, leading to a hallucinated final decision (Spain) and a failure to generate a plausible hypothesis pool.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: The judge failed to detect the lack of consensus (agents were biased to Europe) and did not perform a rigorous check for missing evidence (driving side). It accepted a flawed regional signal without challenging the outlier possibility of the Southern Hemisphere.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool (Spain, Portugal, Greece, Turkey) is completely implausible for the ground truth (New Zealand). It failed to include any Southern Hemisphere candidates despite the visual evidence being consistent with them.
